query: Brain Dump feature for AI context complaints OR frustrating OR broken

results.title: The Brain Dump Technique That Replaced My Entire Productivity System
results.content: and feel about it. The Daily Debrief: End-of-day brain dump about what happened, what worked, what didn't. Going over six minutes: Longer dumps lose focus and create more work for the AI.  Project starts: Context dumps to organize everything I know. Everything else lives in the brain dump system — captured when it matters, organized when I need it. Before brain dumps, my productivity system was a collection of scattered lists, forgotten tasks, and constant low-level anxiety about what I was [...] to schedule that dentist appointment and my mom called yesterday but I was in meetings and now I feel bad about not calling her back and why does everything feel so urgent all the time?". The raw dump captures the emotional chaos and scattered thoughts. Here's something nobody talks about with brain dumps: the emotional release. The voice brain dump externalizes all of that. The Context Dump: Before starting a complex project, do a brain dump about everything you know, think, and feel about it. [...] but I keep thinking about the email from Sarah about the budget meeting and I still haven't responded to that LinkedIn message from the developer who wanted to chat about the project and oh god I completely forgot to submit my expenses and they're due today and I'm also worried that the deployment yesterday might have broken something because I haven't checked the logs and there's this weird bug that Mark mentioned but I don't think I wrote it down anywhere and also I need to schedule that

results.title: [BUG] Persistent Context Broken on Windows - Google Antigravity - Google AI Developers Forum
results.content:
- A small number of brain directories contain user-created artifacts (7 out of 30)

### What is broken

1. \\brain/ directories are mostly empty\\: 24 out of 30 brain directories contain zero files. No `.system\_generated/logs/`, no `overview.txt`, no artifacts. Only 7 directories have any files, and those are user-triggered artifacts (e.g., `bug\_report.md`, media files), not system-generated brain logs. [...] ## Problem

The persistent context system (brain logs, Knowledge Items, context state) is non-functional on Windows. Conversations are saved, but the background processes that build cross-conversation memory never produce output for the vast majority of conversations.

### What works

- Conversation `.pb` files are saved correctly (30 conversations)

- Brain directories are created for each conversation (1:1 match with conversations) [...] ├── knowledge/ → knowledge.lock ONLY (0 Knowledge Items) ✗

├── context\_state/ → EMPTY ✗

├── implicit/ → 7 .pb files (ALL orphaned — no matching conversation) ✗

├── html\_artifacts/

├── playground/

├── mcp\_config.json

├── onboarding.json

└── installation\_id

```

### Brain directory breakdown

| Status | Count | Description |

-------

| Empty | 24 | No files at all |

| User artifacts only | 6 | Contains media (.png) or user-created .md files, no system logs |

results.title: [BUG] Persistent Context Broken on Windows — brain/ mostly empty, zero Knowledge Items, all implicit/ orphaned - Google Antigravity - Google AI Developers Forum
results.content: [BUG] Persistent Context Broken on Windows — brain/ mostly empty, zero Knowledge Items, all implicit/ orphaned - Google Antigravity - Google AI Developers Forum [...] = Get-ChildItem “$env:USERPROFILE\.gemini\antigravity\conversations” -File | % { $\_.BaseName }. Get-ChildItem “$env:USERPROFILE\.gemini\antigravity\implicit” -File | Where-Object { $convIds -notcontains $\_.BaseName }. - `brain//` should contain system-generated logs, overview, and artifacts for each conversation. - `knowledge/` should contain Knowledge Items derived from conversation data. - `implicit/` file IDs should correspond to actual conversations. | [[BUG] Conversation History Not [...] Title: [BUG] Persistent Context Broken on Windows — brain/ mostly empty, zero Knowledge Items, all implicit/ orphaned - Google Antigravity - Google AI Developers Forum

results.title: The Dump — Your brain dump, organized by AI
results.content: it goes. ### AI sorts it. Claude reads the actual meaning of your note and routes it to the right category automatically. ### Find it later. Browse by category, search across everything, or filter by type. ## Built for how your brain actually works. ### Smart categorization. Notes land in Business Ideas, Family Admin, Meal Planning, or whatever folders you've set up — without you lifting a finger. AI understands context, not just keywords. ### Voice, photo, text. Capture any way that's fastest [...] # Too busy living to sit down and organize?. The Dump turns your chaos into searchable, organized notes — automatically. Download on iOS   Use on Web →. Your ideas live across voice memos, screenshots, and scribbled notebooks — never in the same place twice. You keep promising to "get organized" this weekend. You want an assistant who files things for you without changing how you capture. ### Dump it. Voice memo, photo, or text — whatever's in your head. Don't think about where it goes. ### AI [...] way that's fastest in the moment. Say it, snap it, or type it. ### Search everything. Find any note across all categories instantly. ### Your categories. AI learns your folders and routes notes based on what they actually mean. ### Edit & refine. Fix titles, update content, move categories. ## Your best ideas deserve to be remembered.. P.S. The Dump is in beta — it may have rough edges. Be an early adopter and help us build something you'll use forever.
